explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.findings: Sources describe documented external accountability practices in 1 source(s). Additional documentation references related practices.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: This article provides evidence for external_accountability, fairness, governance, oversight, privacy, and transparency. It supports governance and oversight by describing the establishment of an AI governance board tasked with overseeing AI use, validating outputs, and guiding compliance. The article also supports privacy by mentioning the need for privacy protocols and controls, and transparency by detailing various AI platforms and their capabilities, including AI/ML for predictive maintenance and NLP chatbots for tenant services. Furthermore, it touches upon fairness by highlighting the requirement for ethical frameworks and validation to address AI bias.
relevant_sources.title: AI in Commercial Real Estate: Top Use Cases You Need To Know

fairness.findings: Published materials highlight the requirement for ethical frameworks to address AI bias. They also highlight the requirement for validation to address AI bias.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

summary.overall_findings: Prologis' published materials detail various AI platforms and their capabilities, addressing transparency. Additionally, published materials highlight the requirement for ethical frameworks to address AI bias under fairness, and describe the establishment of an AI governance board tasked with overseeing AI use and validating outputs for both oversight and governance. Privacy is addressed through mentions of the need for privacy protocols and controls, while external accountability practices are described in one source. In total, 6 of 7 evaluated pillars have documented evidence, all of which are addressed at the policy level. No qualifying public evidence was found for explainability; this assessment draws on 11 publicly available sources.
